summary: Matched the sysdolphin baselib GX-link setup routine at func_8039069C as GObj_SetupGXLink, moved its implementation into C, exposed it through gobjgxlink.h, and renamed many existing assembly callsites to the new symbol. The C implementation wires an HSD_GObj render callback, gx_link, and render_priority, validates gx_link against HSD_GObjLibInitData.gx_link_max, walks the GX list by priority, and delegates insertion/reordering to GObj_GXReorder.
what_changed: Deleted the old assembly body for asm/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjgxlink.s and added a C implementation of GObj_SetupGXLink in src/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjgxlink.c.
Renamed many assembly callsites from func_8039069C to GObj_SetupGXLink across effects, fighter, game mode, menu, item, interface, text, and stage assembly files.
Added the public prototype void GObj_SetupGXLink(HSD_GObj* gobj, void (*render_cb)(HSD_GObj*, s32), u8 gx_link, u32 priority) to src/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjgxlink.h.
Extended src/sysdolphin/baselib/gobj.h with HSD_GObjLibInitData and included objalloc.h so the embedded HSD_ObjAllocData fields are typed.
Adjusted HSD_GObj.render_cb's second parameter from int to s32, matching the new GObj_SetupGXLink callback signature.
Added gobjgxlink.h includes to stage-related headers such as grtmars.h and grtness.h so code using GX-link setup has the correct declaration.
The new implementation references lbl_804CE380 as HSD_GObjLibInitData, assert strings for gobjgxlink.c and the gx_link_max assertion, and the GX object list globals lbl_804D7820/lbl_804D7824.
smart_moves: Kept the existing GObj_GXReorder helper and made GObj_SetupGXLink responsible only for validation, field assignment, list-position search, and final reorder insertion.
Modeled the render callback as void (*)(HSD_GObj*, s32), then propagated that exact type into HSD_GObj and the public prototype to avoid mismatched function pointer types.
Introduced HSD_GObjLibInitData rather than leaving lbl_804CE380 as an untyped/global blob, which made the gx_link_max check readable and reusable.
Renamed assembly callsites to the decompiled symbol without changing instruction bytes, preserving matching while improving cross-file symbol searchability.

Used the original assert evidence, including the file string gobjgxlink.c and condition string gx_link <= HSD_GObjLibInitData.gx_link_max, to anchor the C control flow and line-number-sensitive behavior.
decomp_lessons: For baselib functions with many unknown callsites, matching one central function can unlock a high-value symbol rename across a large slice of assembly without needing to decompile every caller.

When a matched function depends on a global library-init struct, define only the fields evidenced by accesses and existing embedded types; here gx_link_max and HSD_ObjAllocData fields justified adding HSD_GObjLibInitData.
Callback parameter widths matter for matching and header hygiene; s32 was used instead of plain int in both HSD_GObj.render_cb and GObj_SetupGXLink.
List insertion routines can often be reconstructed by identifying the traversal condition and delegating actual relink operations to an already matched helper such as GObj_GXReorder.
Adding the correct header include at consumers is preferable to relying on implicit declarations, especially when symbol renames propagate into many stage or menu files.
naming_conventions: The nearby existing routine name GObj_GXReorder established the GObj_ prefix and GX-link naming style for this baselib subsystem.
The global library-init struct type was named HSD_GObjLibInitData, consistent with HSD_GObj and HSD_GObjProc type naming.
Fields use lower_snake_case names such as gx_link_max, render_cb, gx_link, render_priority, next_gx, and prev_gx.
Unresolved globals and strings remained address-label based, such as lbl_804CE380, lbl_804084F0, and lbl_80408500.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced assembly branch labels at callsites from bl func_8039069C to bl GObj_SetupGXLink while preserving the same branch encodings shown in the diff.
Removed the standalone assembly implementation after adding the matching C function, indicating the new C emitted compatible code for that routine.
Matched the priority traversal as for (i = lbl_804D7820[gobj->gx_link]; i != NULL && i->render_priority > gobj->render_priority; i = prev) with prev = i->prev_gx inside the loop.
Used the original assert path with __assert(lbl_804084F0, 167, lbl_80408500) to reproduce the debug/assert behavior.
Kept callsite argument order evidenced by assembly: r3 gobj, r4 render callback, r5 gx_link, r6 priority.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or type lbl_804CE380 and complete HSD_GObjLibInitData.
Search for remaining func_8039069C references after PR 229 to confirm all callsites were renamed.
Inspect later gobjgxlink.c work for additional GX-link routines that share lbl_804D7820/lbl_804D7824 traversal patterns.
Check whether the HSD_GObj.render_cb s32 signature is reused consistently in later decompiled render callbacks.
